parsed_review.summary: CP00-932 (risk A, 10 units, RP30.P04.M08.S02-RP30.P04.M09.S03; 7 CandidatePortal HRX slice + 3 AIReviewQueue HRX slice rows) is a descriptor-only HRX-embedded closeout pack and its descriptor-only closeout claim is well-supported. Direct source inspection (Read/Grep) confirms: (1) HRX_CP932_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:678) and HRX_CP932_REQUIREMENTS (registry.js:2571) match the baseline range/risk/unit counts and section rows; (2) createHrxCp932CandidateAiReviewQueueBridgeDescriptor (registry.js:7808) plus the domain row builder (registry.js:2685) and HRX_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (registry.js:65) hold both CandidatePortal and AIReviewQueue to descriptor_only=true with all runtime/record-write/self-service/review-action/final-judgment flags false and no real employee/candidate/payroll data, credentials, or secrets; (3) validators (validators.js:6138/6177/6293) enforce coverage, no-write, no-real-data, and authority-boundary drift, and the validate script (scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs) chains CP931->CP932 bindings deterministically; (4) tests (packages/hrx/test/model.test.js:1841/1861/1883) cover binding/coverage, closed-runtime descriptor, and the committed contract projection (contracts/hrx-people-contract.json latest_pack/latest_projection = CP00-932). Authority boundary is correct: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime_opening deferred to CP00-933; Hermes packet emits_runtime_receipt=false / deterministic_only=true, i.e. control-plane review only, not product runtime. CP178+ hardening checks pass: readiness auth-preflight succeeded with valid non-empty JSON (is_error=false, exit 0), tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob with no forbidden tools, prompt excludes full diff/source, and no invalid/auth-failed/malformed review attempts are recorded. The pack correctly does NOT claim production_ready=true, runtime_ready, or enterprise trust (manifest/inspection/adjudication/command-evidence are consistently in the expected pre-review pending, descriptor-only state). No P0/P1/P2 issues. One non-blocking P3 caveat: command/test exit codes are recorded in command-evidence.json and are not independently re-executable under read-only tools, and the Hermes gate / final validation ladder are still pending; those deterministic gates plus this read-only receipt must complete before the hrx_candidate_ai_review_queue_bridge_descriptor_verified flag is set. blocks_goal_closeout=false: the human/enterprise-trust final-approval boundary is expected and the pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim.

findings.title: Command/test exit codes are agent-reported and Hermes/validation gates remain pending (inherent to read-only review)
findings.details: Source-level boundary enforcement (registry.js descriptor/row builder/attestation, validators.js drift checks, model.test.js assertions, contracts/hrx-people-contract.json projection) was directly inspected via Read/Grep and is internally consistent. However, the recorded command results (node --check, node --test packages/hrx/test/*.test.js, npm run rp30:hrx:validate, all exit_code 0) cannot be re-executed under read-only tools, so their success is agent-reported rather than independently re-verified in this receipt. command-evidence.json also shows hermes_gate.status=pending and gate_outcome=pending_final_validation, and construction-inspection.json shows verdict=PENDING with review_receipt_ref=null. This is the expected pre-review state, not a defect.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping production_ready_flag (hrx_candidate_ai_review_queue_bridge_descriptor_verified), ensure the deterministic validation ladder (npm test, npm run validate, npm run rp30:hrx:validate, closeout-pack/plan validators, git diff --check) and the Hermes H30 gate are executed and recorded by the control-plane, so this read-only Claude receipt is not the sole evidence of command success. Treat that ladder as the authoritative re-execution step.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational / non-blocking. Inherent to the read-only reviewer model; the deterministic validation ladder and Hermes gate are separate control-plane gates that complete the closeout sequence. Does not block pack or goal closeout.
